FUNDAMENTALS

Structuring A Portfolio

💡 Today we're gonna cover one of the most important but also very neglected topics: how to build a solid portfolio. Just like a bridge relies on various materials for strength, a balanced crypto portfolio draws from a variety of assets to weather market fluctuations. By building this bridge wisely, you pave the way for potential growth and navigate the ever-changing financial landscape with confidence.

1. Goals and Risk tolerance analysis

The first thing you need to do before investing and starting to build your crypto portfolio, is to evaluate your future financial goals and your risk tolerance.

In this infographic I summarized all of the things you need to consider before investing.

Let's suppose you're young and living with your parents. You’ll probably have a high-risk tolerance since you have fewer expenses and you have no kids to maintain. Portfolio drops 20%? You should be fine and not panicking. Your youth is a big leverage as your time horizon can be longer than an older investor. This means that you can focus on growing your portfolio over time and also make a few mistakes along the way, which is the natural recipe for learning.

On the other side, if you play it too conservatively, you might not find your financial goals. In any case, whether you're young or old, the goal is to reduce the risks and increase profits. To do so, a crypto portfolio is made of different components, where each of them plays a specific role.

2. The portfolio

Here are the gears:

  • Stablecoins/cash to protect your capital and buy blood
  • Blue chips such as BTC and ETH. A blue chip is a coin that survived at least 2 bear markets with a long term uptrend. (breaking previous ATH)
  • Mid-risk plays to have some volatility but not crazy one. Mid-caps generally have market caps between $1 billion and $10 billion.
  • High-risk plays to catch 10x/50x/100x. Usually, these are the cryptocurrencies with a tiny market cap (millions)

How to build it

I always recommend building a portfolio step by step using the DCA strategy. I know that might be tempting to rush into crypto and make compulsive buys, especially if you're new. But that's the way for a financial disaster. You need to realize that there is no hurry, particularly in a bear market where everything goes slower. This is what you have to do:

  • Be patient
  • Learn daily
  • Accumulate with DCA
  • Enjoy life in the meanwhile

Seems easy right? Trust me, for the majority is not. People in general have no patience as our society is based on short-term rewards and cheap dopamine. That's why 90% of people fail, because they don't have low time preferences. Use DCA to reduce volatility stress which is the primary source of making irrational decisions (jumping from coin to coin and destroy your capital). Good things take time, never forget about it.

Portfolio examples

Let's now see some examples of portfolios analyzing both pros and cons. Each portfolio got different level of risk that, as said before, should be adapted depending on your risk tolerance, time horizon and financial goals.

The Bitcoin maximalist
  • 100% of the net worth in Bitcoin
  • Never gonna sell but instead save dirty fiat money from traditional job. Stacking sats is the mantra.

This is probably the safest portfolio but also quite boring and with few returns unless you bought it at 10$ or you have big constant cashflow to add.

The Barbell strategy

The Barbell strategy has been made by Nassim Taleb for the traditional economy but we can re-adapt it to the crypto world:

  • 90% safe assets like BTC, ETH or cash
  • 10% high risk
  • 0% of mid-risk plays

This is the classic allocation.

Benefits: You can mitigate the risk with solid plays while going degen at the same time and compounding those high-risk plays into safe assets if they start to run.

Cons: You need to handle the volatility caused by the high-risk part knowing that it can also go to zero.

Extra: I changed my allocation to 70% / 30% to play it in a little more aggressive way.

The 50/30/15/5
  • 50% low risk such as BTC or ETH
  • 30% in mid risk like L1/L2 or high caps
  • 15% in high risk coins
  • 5% in super risky assets like microcaps

Benefits:

  • Mitigate the risk by playing high risk/reward while having safe bets such as Bitcoin or cash
  • Potential profits are interesting
  • Well balanced

Cons:

  • Volatility is high
  • Need to be re-balanced quite often (taking profits from high-risk and respecting the original allocation)
The All-inner
  • 100% of the net worth in one project
  • Often it becomes a religion and not an investment

The all-inner feels part of a community and accumulates his favorite project over a long time period because exclusively believes in it.

Benefits:

  • Potential reward is huge

Cons:

  • Tremendous volatility
  • Possible hacks or bugs can happen, ruining everything
  • Not balanced

This is an extreme level of risk, it's a similar approach to gambling. I don’t recommend it.

My suggestion

Focus on building a solid crypto portfolio, instead of a lottery ticket model. That’s what I suggest:

  • Bitcoin
  • Cash
  • 1/2 mid-risk
  • 1/2 degen plays

Asset allocation depends on your risk appetite. “But mate, BTC cannot make me a lot of money”. That's true, but it will save your ass in difficult moments, when everything will start to go bad and looks like a scam. My tip is to always have a portion, also for exploiting the dominance increase/decrease.

3. Seasonality

Your portfolio should be adapted to market seasons. You don’t put on a jumper with 30 degrees outside and a t-shirt while it’s snowing right? The same concept needs to be applied to your portfolio, evaluating market conditions.

Endowment effect

Unfortunately, I see plenty of people falling for a classic mistake that insanely damages their portfolio. “I’m gonna hold these altcoins through the entire bear market because they're the future!”

This is falling for the Endowment effect and not considering seasonality. While it's wise to accumulate solid projects during a bear market, we shouldn't overestimate our investments due to emotional attachment. This becomes an irrational move that blinds us to building a solid portfolio depending on seasonality.

The rule is trying to adapt, if you want to survive.

Here is how you can “dress” your portfolio during the different seasons:

Bull market
  • Less percentage of cash (10/20%?) always hold one.
  • Low/Medium percentage of BTC
  • Medium % of ETH
  • High percentage of altcoins
Bear market
  • Heavy percentage of cash
  • High percentage of BTC or ETH
  • Low percentage of altcoins

4. Market narratives

The market switches between multiple narratives over the cycles that tend to produce the biggest moves. That’s why it’s important to scan all sectors:

  • ETH
  • ETH L2
  • L1
  • Privacy coins
  • Gaming
  • NFT
  • DeFi and its sub-categories
  • AI

Take note that a lot of new trends will probably emerge in the future, so a strategy could be to allocate a small percentage of our portfolio to potential upcoming narratives. In any case, remember that finding them early it's very difficult. If you're not comfortable and don’t want to risk too much, stick to your original allocation. Better safe than sorry.

5. Final tips

  • Don’t diversify too much

It’s nonsense to have millions of coins, you’re just exposing your capital to more risk without following the projects in the correct way. More confusion and more stress. Less potential gains as capital is widely spread across multiple assets. I personally focus on having 3/5 altcoins to concentrate my capital and have better investment management, find your way. Remember: you’re an investor, not a collector.

  • Don’t FOMO into hyped projects

90% of influencers are not your friends. They’re just paid shillers who want to use you as exit liquidity. If someone says you that a coin will shoot a 100x don’t believe him and stick to your plan if you don’t want to lose money. Also, always take a look at the chart. Do not buy something that has a parabolic price action.

  • Have patience

Invest for the long term and trust the process. I know it’s annoying and painful to handle, but ask yourself: Is it better to invest and wait some years or give up and work for your entire life? Time passes anyway.

  • Stake, if conditions are good

Having a coin that doesn’t work for you is a worthless habit. Every single yield is important, even the smallest as in the long term can be translated into a bigger one. If conditions are good, stake at least a portion of your assets. Don’t underestimate the power of compounding and passive income.

  • Track your portfolio

You need to take note of your progress over time if you want to improve your investor abilities. I personally use “Delta Tracker” but there are other good tools out there. Find your personal tracker and track your journey.

  • Take profits

Building a portfolio is the first step, the second one is taking profits. You’re in this space to make money and not for charity purposes. Make sure to avoid the mistake of leaving money on the table. Don’t be greedy.

  • Don’t underestimate your blue chips

Never ever sell your blue chips to chase pumps or other coins, it's a very risky and potentially self-destroying practice. Blue chips are your edge for the future, treat them with respect and they will treat you in the same manner.
